steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the structure of the passage: it opens with a specific historical example (Jennie Carter's 1867 journalism), then describes an organization that preserves and honors that history (the NABJ).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — “a general overview of Black journalism followed by a specific example”: the passage does the opposite — it opens with Carter's 1867 journalism as the specific example first, then broadens to the NABJ as the preserving organization. The order is reversed. ✗
  • Choice B — “a specific achievement in Black journalistic history followed by a description of an organization dedicated to upholding that history”: matches exactly — Carter's 1867 work is the specific achievement; the NABJ, founded to honor journalists like Carter, is the organization. ✓
  • Choice C — “a historical problem in Black journalism followed by efforts to solve it”: the passage presents no problem to be solved; it celebrates Carter's achievement and the organization that honors it. ✗
  • Choice D — “competing perspectives on the significance of Black journalistic history”: the passage presents one coherent perspective, not competing viewpoints. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
